name: Vastmanland County (Sweden)

colors.symbolism: the silver (argent) field of the provincial arms

description: A square white (argent) banner charged centrally with the historic Vastmanland provincial arms, depicting flames issuing from rocks (or a burning grate), referring to the region's iron-mining heritage.
history: The flag is the heraldic banner of the arms of Vastmanland County, derived from the coat of arms of the historic province of Vastmanland; the burning rocks symbolize the province's traditional iron-mining and ironworking industries.

features.symbolism: the silver (argent) field of the historic Vastmanland provincial arms

features.role: Vastmanland County arms (three rocks with flames)
features.symbolism: three burning rocks (or a fire-grate over rocks) in gold and red on the silver field, an emblem alluding to the iron-mining and metallurgical traditions of the province of Vastmanland.
